item.title: Carole King

item.title: Dionne Warwick
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, standards Scene: Brill Building Era: 1962–present (primary period: 1962–1971) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Listen to the way Dionne Warwick navigates the melody of “Walk on By” — the intervals are wide, and the phrasing syncopates against Burt Bacharach’s rhythm track in ways that m...

item.title: Dusty Springfield
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, blue-eyed soul, soul Scene: London; Memphis; New York Era: 1958–1999 (primary period: 1963–1969) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Mary Isobel Catherine Bernadette O’Brien, from West Hampstead, London, became Dusty Springfield and sang Black American music with a devotion and a vocal int...

item.title: Elvis Presley
item.description: Genres: Rock & roll, rockabilly, rhythm & blues, pop, country, gospel Scene: Memphis (Sun Records, RCA) Era: 1954–1977 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The voice was the instrument.

item.title: Laura Nyro
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, singer-songwriter, progressive soul Scene: Brill Building (adjacent), New York City Era: 1966–1997 (primary period: 1967–1971) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The piano on “Timer” (1968) shifts from a ballad’s tenderness to a percussive, almost violent intensity in the space of a breat...

item.title: Marvin Gaye
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul, psychedelic soul, quiet storm Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1961–1984 (primary period: 1964–1973) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Listen to the shift between “I Heard It Through the Grapevine” (1968) and What’s Going On (1971): from a single built on paranoia and rhythmic ...

item.title: Muddy Waters
item.description: Genres: Chicago blues, Electric blues, Delta blues Scene: Chicago (Chess Records) Era: 1948–1958 (Chess peak); active to 1983 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The voice is unhurried and half-spoken, sure of itself to the point of menace, a man stating his business and daring the room to disagree.

item.title: George Martin
item.description: Era: 1950s–1998 (primary period: 1962–1970) Genres: Pop rock, Merseybeat, psychedelic pop, art rock, baroque pop Key artists: The Beatles RYM · Apple Music · Spotify George Martin signed the Beatles because nobody else would, and the records they made together over the next eight years changed what ...

item.title: Phil Spector
